page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, delivering 8 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core. The x86_64 architecture is supported by a 35 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The instance is configured with 32 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between multi-core and single-core capabilities. The ecs.g5.2xlarge achieves average multi-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 5881.5 and a PassMark CPU score of 7041.88. Its memory bandwidth is also average, reaching 40.9 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1006.0 and a low single-threaded gzip compression rate. While it handles small-model LLM inference and Redis SET operations with average efficiency, performance drops significantly on larger workloads, such as medium 7B LLM inference.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g5.2xlarge offers a stable, dedicated resource profile for standard enterprise applications that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention, though the older processor architecture limits its efficiency for modern, instruction-heavy tasks. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, Redis caching, and moderate multi-threaded application servers.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 32 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 server features a 2.5 GHz clock speed, a 35 MB L3 cache, and supports AVX-512 instructions. It includes 2 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, solid Redis SET operations at 5.8 million operations per second, and stable static web serving throughput at 6.22 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and memory scores fall into the weaker percentiles, and medium LLM inference is poor. This instance is best suited for balanced general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and caching applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose KVM-based instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for web serving and caching.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute instance offering balanced memory-to-CPU ratios for standard web serving and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
32 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Average multi-core performance and 5.8 million Redis SET operations/sec
Weak single-core performance and limited efficiency for large LLM tasks
Best suited for static web serving, caching, and general-purpose workloads
